Dircaea palustris, commonly known as Leatherwood, is a shrub native to eastern North America, known for its woody stem and small, white flowers. The health benefits of Dircaea palustris include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
